data.title: ZenaTech set to share its progress in global drone services and defense sector at three investor conferences.
data.description: ZenaTech will offer insights on its expansion of Global Drone as a Service and advancements in the defense sector at three upcoming investor conferences. The company will discuss its strategies for growth and provide updates on key metrics, including revenue and market penetration, highlighting the opportunities within the defense industry. Attendees can expect to learn about ZenaTech's plans for scaling its drone services globally and its focus on innovation to meet the evolving needs of the market.

data.title: Curaleaf opens 71st store in Florida, topping 160 nationwide. Leading cannabis provider.
data.description: Curaleaf Holdings, Inc., a prominent global cannabis provider, has expanded its presence in Florida to a total of 71 locations. This growth reflects the company's broader strategy to bolster its nationwide footprint, which now exceeds 160 stores with the latest opening. The new store in Stamford, Conn. launched on Jan. 15, 2026. Curaleaf's shares trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol CURA and on the OTCQX market under the symbol CURLF.

data.title: IHC and U.S. DFC collaborate to mobilize capital in critical sectors like minerals, energy, healthcare, etc.
data.description: IHC and the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) have established a strategic partnership to facilitate the mobilization of capital in key sectors including critical minerals, energy, logistics, healthcare, food security, and Information, Communication, and Technology (ICT). This framework will focus on joint origination efforts to promote investment in these sectors.

data.title: Short squeeze affects top 500 cryptos with largest squeeze since October. Bitcoin outperforms USD amid geopolitical uncertainty.
data.description: The cryptocurrency markets experienced a significant short squeeze, the biggest one since October. This resulted in the liquidation of short positions as Bitcoin surged in value compared to the US dollar. This sudden spike in Bitcoin's performance occurred against a backdrop of geopolitical uncertainty in the market.

data.title: Online casinos are seeking new ways to engage players through Web3 incentives, moving from passive promotions to mission-based rewards. High Roller Technologies is collaborating with Power Protocol to explore innovative engagement models, aiming to enhance retention and generate incremental revenue. The industry is shifting towards personalized, immersive experiences to meet evolving consumer expectations.
data.description: Online casinos are exploring new ways to engage players beyond traditional methods such as bonuses and loyalty points. High Roller Technologies, Inc. (NYSE: ROLR) is partnering with Power Protocol to experiment with mission-based rewards and behavioral incentives to enhance player engagement. While traditional methods may drive short-term activity, they often fail to foster long-term loyalty. Players now seek more personalized and interactive experiences, leading operators to consider innovative approaches to retain users. This shift towards behavioral and mission-based incentives aims to strengthen the connection between operators and players while increasing retention and revenue. The article emphasizes the importance of evolving engagement strategies in the competitive online casino industry.

data.title: Coinbase CEO withdraws support for Senate crypto bill over stablecoin rewards ban, forcing markup postponement. Legislation faces challenges beyond stablecoins, including tokenized securities ban and DeFi surveillance expansion. Stock trading at $252.
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong surprised Washington by withdrawing support for the Senate Banking Committee's crypto market structure bill, causing the postponement of the markup indefinitely. The bill proposed restrictions on stablecoin rewards for users, which Armstrong viewed as a threat to Coinbase's strategy. The legislation also aimed to ban tokenized securities and expand surveillance on DeFi transactions. Coinbase stock traded around $252, down from its high of $444, amid regulatory uncertainty. Armstrong's objections highlight fundamental disputes in the bill, leading to skepticism that issues can be resolved by the rescheduled markup in January. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Hundreds of Oklahoma students promote school choice at Dove Science Academy's South Middle/High School in 2 weeks.
data.description: In Oklahoma City on January 15, 2026, numerous students from various locations in Oklahoma are set to assemble at Dove Science Academy's South Middle / High School to express their admiration for their educational institutions. The gathering will represent a significant number of K-12 school choice alternatives available in the Sooner State.

data.title: Del Monte Foods secures successful bidders in auction process for all business segments, focusing on quality food products.
data.description: Del Monte Foods has finalized asset purchase agreements for all its business segments, such as Vegetable, Fruit, Tomato, and Broth & Stock units. The company's priority is to maintain the production of top-notch food items during the transition period. This significant development was announced on January 15, 2026, in Walnut Creek, California.

data.title: BlackRock's total assets hit $14 trillion, revenue grew 23% to $7.01B, and adjusted EPS rose 10% to $13.16 in Q4 2025.
data.description: BlackRock, Inc. (NYSE: BLK) saw a surge in stock prices following the release of its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results. The company reported a 23% year-over-year revenue growth to $7.01 billion, surpassing the expected $6.69 billion. Adjusted operating income also rose by 22% Y/Y to $2.85 billion. Adjusted EPS increased by 10% Y/Y to $13.16, beating the projected $12.25. The revenue breakdown shows that investment advisory, administration fees, and securities lending revenue reached $5.28 billion from last year's $4.42 billion, driven by various factors. Securities lending revenue increased to $174 million from $161 million, primarily due to wider spreads. Investment advisory performance fees spiked to $754 million from $451 million.

data.title: Mount Sinai offers MRI-guided prostate treatment with TULSA-PRO®, preserving functions, first in Metro NY.
data.description: Mount Sinai is making strides in prostate care by implementing an innovative AI-powered therapy. This cutting-edge approach is aimed at preserving both continence and sexual function in patients. With an emphasis on patient outcomes and quality of life, this new therapy has shown promising results. By integrating artificial intelligence technology into prostate care, Mount Sinai is revolutionizing treatment options for patients, improving overall quality of care.

data.title: Bladex (NYSE: BLX) to hold Virtual Investor Day on March 24, 2026, established by 23 central banks in Latin America & Caribbean.
data.description: Announced on January 15, 2026, in Panama City, multinational bank Bladex (NYSE: BLX) founded by 23 central banks of Latin-American and Caribbean nations to enhance foreign trade and economic synergy in the area, revealed its plans.

data.title: Zimmer Biomet to host Q4 2025 earnings call on Feb 10 at 8:30 am ET. Leader in medical technology.
data.description: In Warsaw, Indiana, on January 15, 2026, Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. (NYSE and SIX: ZBH), a prominent medical technology company, revealed that their upcoming fourth quarter earnings conference call will be streamed online on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time. The conference call aims to provide insight into the company's recent financial performance.
